notes: Three query categories: iconic_phrase has a known source book the query is taken from verbatim or near-verbatim. topical has no single source but should resonate in a domain. adversarial should NOT score high anywhere (noise-floor probe).

iconic_phrase.query: the unexamined life is not worth living
iconic_phrase.notes: The 2026-05-17 baseline measured this missing top-20 entirely.

iconic_phrase.query: I think, therefore I am
from my grandfather Verus I learned good morals
some things are in our power and others are not
the cave with prisoners chained watching shadows on the wall
philosophy is preparation for death
act only according to that maxim which you can will to be a universal law
iconic_phrase.source: Kant, Groundwork / Critique of Practical Reason

iconic_phrase.query: God is dead and we have killed him
iconic_phrase.source: Nietzsche, Thus Spake Zarathustra / Gay Science

iconic_phrase.query: human life solitary poor nasty brutish and short
iconic_phrase.source: Hobbes, Leviathan — NOT in corpus, recall baseline for missing source

iconic_phrase.query: happiness is an activity of the soul in accordance with virtue
what is past is done with
the only thing I know is that I know nothing
when you arise in the morning think of what a privilege it is to be alive
everything that happens happens as it should
a treatise of human nature being an attempt to introduce the experimental method
iconic_phrase.source: Hume, Treatise of Human Nature (verbatim subtitle)

iconic_phrase.query: the will to power
iconic_phrase.source: Nietzsche — corpus has pg52915 'Will to Power Book III-IV' specifically (high-volume target)

iconic_phrase.query: every action of yours that has no reference to a social end
no man steps in the same river twice
iconic_phrase.source: Heraclitus — fragment, not a complete book; recall baseline

iconic_phrase.query: appearances are not realities and what is is not what seems
iconic_phrase.source: Plato — recurring motif across Republic, Phaedo, Theaetetus

iconic_phrase.query: concerning the principles of human knowledge
iconic_phrase.source: Berkeley, Principles of Human Knowledge (verbatim title)

topical.notes: Should land in Aristotle + Maimonides territory.

topical.query: scepticism and the limits of knowledge
the will and freedom of choice

adversarial.notes: High-frequency token spam. Top similarity should be < 0.65.
Nonsense ASCII. Top similarity should be < 0.50.
Empty query. Should error gracefully, NOT return high-similarity hits.
Single common word. Top similarity should be in the 0.55–0.65 range — present but not confident.
Emoji + filler. Tests Unicode handling. Similarity should be diffuse.
